title: Cannot Set '--memory' (cgroup) on Rootless Podman, but '--cpuset-cpus' (cgroup) Works
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
After setting up rootless podman, I wanted to get cgroups working. From what I've read, cgroups with rootless podman is really only recommended on cgroups v2. As I'm running Ubuntu 20.04, this has meant disabling cgroups v1 via kernel commandline, enabling Delegation to my user of some additional cgroups to my user (slice), and allowing my user permissions to the unified cgroup hierarchy. So far, so good. Podman has also been configured to use fuse-overlayfs and a few other minor changes to enable rootless.

Firing up a container like this works:
`podman run -d --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --cpuset-cpus=03 --security-opt seccomp=/home/.../seccomp_default.json --cap-drop=cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setpcap --name=firefox -p 5800:5800 -p 5900:5900 -v ./docker/appdata/firefox:/config:rw --shm-size 4g jlesage/firefox`

I am able to see the libpod_parent cgroup hierarchy created and the delegated controls are being set. However, this fails:

`podman run -d --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --memory=2000m --security-opt seccomp=/home/.../seccomp_default.json --cap-drop=cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setpcap --name=firefox -p 5800:5800 -v ./docker/appdata/firefox:/config:rw --shm-size 4g jlesage/firefox`

with error:

`Error: opening file `memory.swap.max` for writing: Permission denied: OCI runtime permission denied error`

The particular container doesn't seem to matter and the problem appears to be related very specifically to the error message, as there is no memory.swap.max control in the cgroup hierarchy. I am actually torn as to whether this is a kernel bug or podman, since the kernel docs for my kernel, an Ubuntu 5.4.x series, seems to indicate that this should be a valid control, but it's never mapped into the group with the other controls.

Here is the structure for my current running pod:

```ls -v /sys/fs/cgroup/podman/libpod_parent/libpod-2f65d17a899ad2beb22167dcfe0ab130ed2214685d5d6137593b91d8eae2e83d/
cgroup.controllers
cgroup.events
cgroup.freeze
cgroup.max.depth
cgroup.max.descendants
cgroup.procs
cgroup.stat
cgroup.threads
cgroup.type
cgroup.subtree_control
cpu.max
cpu.pressure
cpu.stat
cpu.uclamp.max
cpu.uclamp.min
cpu.weight
cpu.weight.nice
cpuset.cpus
cpuset.cpus.effective
cpuset.cpus.partition
cpuset.mems
cpuset.mems.effective
io.max
io.pressure
io.stat
io.weight
memory.current
memory.events
memory.events.local
memory.high
memory.low
memory.max
memory.min
memory.oom.group
memory.pressure
memory.stat
pids.current
pids.events
pids.max
```

The file memory.swap.max simply isn't there. I've upgraded podman, crun, and my kernel and rebuilt it, just in case... The fact that the other controls work seems to be a strong indicator that the hierarchy simply is not what is expected for cgroups v2, at least according to the docs and Podman's behavior. Some other info:

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: @giuseppe Didn't we merge something recently to disable swap limits on v2 if they're not supported by the kernel? I imagine this would resolve this?
@daveman1010221 If you could possibly try master, this may be resolved.

Building/installing podman from master has caused some new issues to track down. It's ignoring everything in my home directory now and gets permission denied on /var/run/containers. I installed using the build directions for source. Maybe that's not right for running rootless?

Running mainline has me chasing down problems that I would not have otherwise, including this one:

```
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
```

I am unable to verify whether or not mainline solves the swap limit problem.

That is harmless - it's an expected warning when running roootless, you'll see it on 2.1.1 as well. We should probably get around to fixing it to not print if we're not root, but since it harms nothing it's low priority.
Can you provide details on the other issues you're seeing?

I'm really not sure where to start, I can tell you that my system is not in what I would consider a sane state. I had to give my user permissions to folders it would not normally have permissions to, in order to even get it to run. Mainline will not allow for the overlayfs to live in my home folder anymore, it insists in being in /var/lib/containers/storage. It completely ignores my ~/.config/containers directory, even if I completely remove all other configs. So, it's a hack that it's working at all at this point.

Your Podman appears to believe it's running as root, despite being rootless. Can you provide a `podman info`? Also, `printenv` might help, to see if there are any strange XDG_ variables set. Any environment variables starting with `_CONTAINERS` could be causing us to believe we're not rootless.

Alright. We are definitely running rootless, which makes the use of those paths very interesting.

Can you run `podman system reset` (warning: will remove all containers and images to reset state) and remove any `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` files that may exist?

Removed all ~/.config/containers/*

Make anything of this error message?

```
podman system reset                                                                                                                            Fri 13 Nov 2020 07:20:12 PM UTC

WARNING! This will remove:
        - all containers
        - all pods
        - all images
        - all build cache
Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
ERRO[0001] Error registering shutdown handler for libpod: handler with name libpod already exists
```

Ok, it's running again. :-D That system reset seems to have cleared something out. Also, I am not getting the swap error now, when setting --memory limits, which is very good. Just need to figure out why it isn't allowing overlay storage in my home directory now.

This is a beautiful thing:

```
cat /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-4dd787bb99b99a48d5ce8cf4920515bf783bfd4377249e1dade7145ad625ddf9/memory.max                                Fri 13 Nov 2020 07:39:06 PM UTC
2097152000
```
which is exactly the 2GB limit I set. :+1:
It seems the memory.swap.max issue is resolved in mainline. Thanks. And thanks for helping me to get a running podman again. :-D
